mainEntity.name: How much milk does a goat produce per day?

acceptedAnswer.text: Saanen goats produce 1-1.5 gallons per day at peak. Alpine and LaMancha average 0.75-1 gallon. Nubian goats give 0.5-0.75 gallons of rich, high-butterfat milk. Nigerian Dwarf goats produce 1-2 quarts per day.

mainEntity.name: How long is a goat's lactation cycle?

acceptedAnswer.text: A typical goat lactation cycle is 10 months (305 days). Production peaks at 4-6 weeks after kidding, then gradually declines. Some does can be milked through without rebreeding for extended lactation.

mainEntity.name: How much cheese does a gallon of goat milk make?

acceptedAnswer.text: One gallon of goat milk yields approximately 1 pound of hard cheese or 1.5-2 pounds of soft cheese (chevre). Goat milk has slightly less casein than cow milk, so cheese yields are a bit lower.

mainEntity.name: What goat breed produces the most milk?

acceptedAnswer.text: Saanen goats are the top producers at 1-1.5 gallons per day. Alpine and LaMancha goats are close behind. For small homesteads, Nigerian Dwarf goats produce the most milk relative to their size with high butterfat.

mainEntity.name: How much goat milk for a batch of soap?

acceptedAnswer.text: A typical goat milk soap recipe uses about 10-12 ounces (1.25-1.5 cups) of goat milk per pound of oils, which makes about 4-6 bars. One gallon of milk makes roughly 8-10 batches of soap.

mainEntity.name: When does goat milk production peak?

acceptedAnswer.text: Milk production peaks at 4-6 weeks after kidding, then gradually declines over the 10-month lactation. Production drops faster after month 7. First-fresheners produce about 70-80% of mature doe production.

howItWorks:
How the Goat Milk Calculator Works

This calculator estimates milk production based on breed averages and lactation curve adjustments.

Daily Milk = Does × Breed Avg × Lactation Factor
  • Saanen: 1.25 gal/day peak, highest volume breed
  • Nigerian Dwarf: 0.375 gal/day peak, highest butterfat (6.5%)
  • Lactation curve: Early 80%, peak 100%, mid 75%, late 50%
  • Cheese: ~1 lb hard cheese per gallon of milk
  • Soap: ~10-12 oz milk per batch (4-6 bars)
faqHTML:
Frequently Asked Questions

How much milk does a goat produce per day?

Saanen goats lead at 1-1.5 gallons/day at peak. Alpine and LaMancha average 0.75-1 gallon. Nubian goats give 0.5-0.75 gallons of rich milk. Nigerian Dwarf goats produce 1-2 quarts daily.

How long is a goat's lactation cycle?

A typical lactation is 10 months (305 days). Production peaks at 4-6 weeks after kidding and gradually declines. Some does can be milked through for extended lactation without rebreeding.

How much cheese does a gallon of goat milk make?

One gallon yields about 1 pound of hard cheese or 1.5-2 pounds of soft cheese like chevre. Higher-butterfat breeds (Nubian, Nigerian Dwarf) produce better cheese yields per gallon.

What goat breed produces the most milk?

Saanens are the top producers at 1-1.5 gallons/day. For small homesteads, Nigerian Dwarfs offer the best milk-to-size ratio with the highest butterfat content (6.5%), ideal for cheese.

How much goat milk for a batch of soap?

A typical batch uses 10-12 ounces of goat milk per pound of oils, producing 4-6 bars. One gallon of milk can supply roughly 8-10 soap batches, making it a great use for surplus milk.

When does goat milk production peak?

Production peaks at 4-6 weeks after kidding. It holds relatively steady through month 3-4, then gradually declines. By month 7-10, production is about 40-60% of peak output.
